events.title: Naturalist Night Out: Owls
events.description: Join us for a themed stroll under the World’s Largest Urban Night Sky Place.
events.sourceName: Forest Preserves of Cook County Events

events.title: Wheaton Independence Day Fireworks 2026 at Graf Park (July 3)
events.description: Celebrate America's birthday in Wheaton with a free fireworks show at Graf Park on Friday, July 3, 2026. The Wheaton Park District's Independence Day Celebration features family activities, music, and giveaways before fireworks light up the sky around 9 p.m.

events.title: Palatine Hometown Fest Fireworks 2026 (July 3)
events.description: Kick off the Fourth of July weekend at the Palatine Jaycees Hometown Fest, where free fireworks light up Community Park at dusk on Friday, July 3, 2026. The family-friendly festival runs July 2-5 with carnival rides, live music, food, and games for all ages.

events.title: Wheaton Independence Day Parade 2026 (July 4)
events.description: Downtown Wheaton hosts its annual Independence Day parade at 10 a.m. on Saturday, July 4, 2026. This free, family-friendly tradition features floats, marching bands, and community entries celebrating the Fourth of July.

events.title: Palatine Independence Day Parade 2026 at Hometown Fest (July 4)
events.description: Celebrate the Fourth of July in Palatine with the Hometown Fest Independence Day parade at 10:30 a.m. on Saturday, July 4, 2026. The family-friendly festival at Community Park continues all day with rides, music, food, and games.

events.title: Puerto Rican Arts Alliance: Studio Arts Program
events.description: El Rinconcito is a free open art space for creative expression for people ages 12-25. You can meet people like you who are interested in arts, painting, drawing, printmaking, and others. El Riconcito provides a safe and welcoming studio for art making. An artist and a counselor in the studio can guide you in creative experiences, but you can opt to do your o

events.title: Evanston 4th of July Parade 2026 (July 4)
events.description: Join Evanston's beloved Independence Day parade on Saturday, July 4, 2026, stepping off at 2 p.m. along Central Street toward Ryan Field. This free, family-friendly tradition features floats, marching bands, and community groups for all ages.

events.title: Constructing Jathi Choreography with Harini Nilakantan
events.description: Weekly Saturdays 1:15 – 2:15 PM Harini Nilakantan is a student of Aacharya Choodamani Guru Smt. Roja Kannan, and has over two decades of experience in this art form. In this 90-minute class, dancers will learn new choreography – both how to piece together combinations and increase retention with her for advanced rhythmic patterns or Jathis. Class focus: exec

events.title: Evanston Lakefront Concert & Fireworks 2026 (July 4)
events.description: Cap off the Fourth of July in Evanston with a free lakefront concert and fireworks at Dawes Park on Saturday, July 4, 2026. The Palatine Concert Band plays from 7:30 to 9 p.m., with fireworks over Lake Michigan at approximately 9:30 p.m.

events.title: Naperville Community Fireworks Show 2026 (July 4)
events.description: Watch a free Independence Day fireworks show in Naperville at the Frontier Sports Complex beginning at 9:30 p.m. on Saturday, July 4, 2026. Admission and parking are free, and the Naperville Municipal Band performs patriotic classics at 7:30 p.m. beforehand.

events.title: Navy Pier Independence Day Fireworks 2026 in Chicago (July 4)
events.description: Ring in the Fourth of July with free fireworks over Lake Michigan at Navy Pier in Chicago on Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 10 p.m. The choreographed show celebrates Independence Day against the city's iconic skyline, and no tickets are required.

events.title: Summer Writing Institute for Teachers
events.description: Join us for a professional development session co-presented by Teach Tank . This professional development session will include brand-new creative writing exercises designed to spark stimulating conversations with a focus on making new connections and learning from one another. events.title: The DuPage County Fair
events.description: July 23-26, 2026 The DuPage County Fair is one of Wheaton’s highlighted community events. We love seeing all community members come together to enjoy quality time with their neighbors, friends, and family. events.title: 4th Annual Mela
events.description: Kalapriya’s annual Mela is back! Join us for a fantastic day of fun with South Asian music, dance, food, and crafts! Experience incredible performances by local, national, and international musicians and dancers, taste some authentic food, enjoy arts and crafts, shop at local retail vendors, and wear some body art from henna artists! events.title: Constructing Jathi Choreography with Harini Nilakantan
events.description: Weekly Saturdays 1:15 – 2:15 PM Harini Nilakantan is a student of Aacharya Choodamani Guru Smt. Roja Kannan, and has over two decades of experience in this art form. In this 90-minute class, dancers will learn new choreography – both how to piece together combinations and increase retention with her for advanced rhythmic patterns or Jathis. Class focus: exec

events.title: Pandit Uday Bhawalkar Live at SAI
events.description: Dhrupad Immersion: Pandit Uday Bhawalkar Live at SAI Saturday, July 11, 2026 South Asia Institute is pleased to welcome internationally acclaimed Dhrupad maestro Uday Bhawalkar for a rare evening of immersive Indian classical music. Rooted in one of South Asia’s oldest vocal traditions, the performance invites audiences into a contemplative sonic experience
events.venue: South Asia Institute 1925 South Michigan Avenue Chicago, IL 60616

events.title: Insect Fest
events.description: Join us for this all-things-insect event! Go on a bug hunt, experience an insect petting zoo and more!
events.sourceName: Forest Preserves of Cook County Events

events.title: Picturing Mexico: Architecture & Modernism
events.description: In partnership with the Edith Farnsworth House, architecture scholars Luis E. Carranza and Kathryn O’Rourke will explore ten photographs that shaped modern architecture and urban planning in Mexico. In Mexico, architects used photography to stay connected to global design movements and communicate the ideas behind their work. Photographers, including Armando

events.title: Haitian Heritage Month Konpa Sware
events.description: Join the Haitian American Museum of Chicago (HAMOC) at its monthly Konpa Sware! The museum is excited to continue this immersive event which includes an evening of Haitian dance and music. Join us at Room43. Food will be available for free and the FIRST 50 GUESTS will recieve 1 FREE DRINK! FEATURING MUSIC BY: DJ Frantz! This is a recurring event on the Third

events.title: 3rd Fridays Free Admission Museum Night
events.description: Free Admission Museum Nights take place every third Friday of each month from 5 pm to 8 pm! The tour will start at 6 PM on time. All tours depart from the reception desk, and please be sure to arrive on time. The event offers wine and appetizers. This Friday at 7-7:40 PM, our Resident Artist 2024 – Irene Hsiao will perform at the Heritage Museum of Asian Art

events.title: Stammtisch Open Haus, Every 3rd Friday
events.description: Feiern Sie mit uns! Our monthly Open Haus Stammtisch is a great way to meet new friends and enjoy a little Gemütlichkeit over some delicious German food and drink! events.description: An evening designed to empower, connect and inspire. Led by Gayle Byck of InTune with Nature. Cost: $10/person. Ages 18 & up.

events.title: Hummingbird Trivia
